title: Criminal Background Certificate Cost 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Criminal Background Certificate Cost & Processing Times
description: Criminal background certificate cost in Argentina 2026. Compare standard (5 days), urgent (24h), and express (3h) delivery options.
intro: Official government procedures in Argentina have varying costs by jurisdiction, processing times, and required documentation. Knowing these details upfront saves you trips and unexpected surprises. Our criminal background certificate calculator covers standard and express options updated for 2026. All values are current and reviewed periodically to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Standard National Registry Fee**. Direct application using your selected processing option. Criminal background certificate costs vary by speed: standard, urgent, and express options available for 2026.

explanation:
Official government procedures in Argentina have specific costs, timelines, and requirements set by each agency (RENAPER, DNRPA, AFIP, Migraciones, Registro Civil, etc.). This calculator estimates the total cost and necessary documents for obtaining a criminal background certificate.

## How It's Calculated

The calculator applies the standard National Registry fee based on your selected processing speed. Different processing options (standard, urgent, express) have different costs and timelines, allowing you to choose based on your needs and budget.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for informational purposes only. For critical decisions involving employment, visa applications, or legal matters, consult with a qualified professional. All values are current for 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is a criminal background certificate (Certificado de Antecedentes Penales)?
faq.a: It's an official document from Argentina's National Reincidence Registry (Registro Nacional de Reincidencia) under the Ministry of Justice that certifies your criminal history status. It's required for employment, visa applications, and professional licenses.

faq.q: How much does a criminal background certificate cost?
faq.a: Costs vary by processing speed: Standard (5 days) costs approximately ARS 30,000; Urgent (24 hours) is higher; Express (3 hours) has the highest cost. Current rates should be verified on the official website.

faq.q: How long is the certificate valid?
faq.a: Criminal background certificates are valid for 30 days from the issue date. For work or visa purposes, validity can be extended to 90 days with an additional request.
Yes. Applications are processed online at www.dnrec.jus.gov.ar using a digital signature, or you can schedule an in-person appointment through argentina.gob.ar.

faq.q: How long does it take to receive the certificate?
faq.a: Processing times depend on your selected option: Standard (5 business days), Urgent (24 hours), or Express (3 hours). Times are calculated from the application submission date.
For most applications, yes. You can book an appointment through argentina.gob.ar or your jurisdiction's official website. Emergency cases may be processed without an appointment.

faq.q: What documents do I need to apply?
faq.a: Typically required: your national ID (DNI) original and photocopy, CUIT certificate, and proof of residence. Some jurisdictions may require additional documents based on specific circumstances.

faq.q: What is the National Reincidence Registry?
faq.a: It's the official government database maintained by Argentina's Ministry of Justice that tracks criminal records and prior convictions for legal, employment, and administrative purposes.
